# Quick CLI

Use the command line to deploy quick sites (https://quick.shopify.io)

## Installation

```bash
npm install -g @shopify/quick
```

## Usage

```bash
# Deploy a directory to Quick hosting
quick deploy ./my-site my-awesome-project

# Your site will be live at:
# https://my-awesome-project.quick.shopify.io
```

## Requirements

- Node.js 14+
- Google Cloud CLI (`gcloud`)
- Authenticated Google Cloud session

## Commands

### `quick deploy <dir> <name>`

Deploy a directory to Quick hosting.

**Arguments:**

- `dir` - Directory to deploy (must contain index.html)
- `name` - Subdomain name for your site

**Options:**

- `-f, --force` - Overwrite existing site without confirmation

**Examples:**

```bash
# Deploy current directory
quick deploy . my-site

# Deploy specific directory
quick deploy ./dist my-production-site

# Force overwrite existing site
quick deploy ./build my-site --force
```

## Authentication

Make sure you're authenticated with Google Cloud:

```bash
gcloud auth login
```

## Support

- 🐛 [Report issues](https://shopify.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0962U5ENF8)
- 📖 [Slack Channel](https://shopify.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0962U5ENF8)

## Deploying a new version

```
git config --global push.followTags true
npm version minor  #(or major / patch)
git push
```

This package is then hosted on our internal private Cloudsmith package hosting.
To kick off a build so it shows up on cloudsmith, do it manually with a new build in BuildKite
https://buildkite.com/shopify/quick

Made with ⚡ by Shopify
